---
title: "Conway's Game of Life"
subtitle: "You found it."
page-layout: full
toc: false
sidebar: false
---

Four [trivial rules](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conway%27s_Game_of_Life#Rules), applied to every cell at once, produce unbounded complexity. From them you can build oscillators, mobile 'spaceships', logic gates, glider guns that emit them, and a working universal Turing machine.

Click or drag to paint. The grid wraps at the edges.

```{=html}
<div class="life-app">
  <div class="life-controls sim-controls">
    <button id="life-play" type="button">Play</button>
    <button id="life-step" type="button">Step</button>
    <button id="life-clear" type="button">Clear</button>
    <button id="life-random" type="button">Randomize</button>
    <select id="life-preset" aria-label="Preset pattern">
      <option value="">Preset…</option>
      <option value="glider">Glider</option>
      <option value="gun">Gosper glider gun</option>
      <option value="pulsar">Pulsar</option>
      <option value="lwss">Lightweight spaceship</option>
    </select>
    <label class="life-speed">
      Speed
      <input id="life-fps" type="range" min="2" max="30" step="1" value="12">
      <span id="life-fps-out">12</span>/s
    </label>
  </div>
  <canvas id="life-canvas" class="sim-canvas" aria-label="Game of Life simulation"></canvas>
</div>

<style>
  .life-app {
    display: flex;
    flex-direction: column;
    gap: 0.75rem;
    margin: 1rem 0 2rem;
  }
  .life-speed {
    display: inline-flex;
    align-items: center;
    gap: 0.4rem;
    margin-left: 0.5rem;
  }
  .life-speed input { accent-color: #2ea043; }  /* green = "alive", a deliberate per-widget accent */
  #life-canvas {
    background: var(--bs-body-bg);
    image-rendering: pixelated;
    cursor: crosshair;
  }
</style>

<script>
(() => {
  const COLS = 80, ROWS = 50, CELL = 10;
  const ALIVE = '#2ea043';

  const canvas = document.getElementById('life-canvas');
  const ctx = canvas.getContext('2d');
  canvas.width = COLS * CELL;
  canvas.height = ROWS * CELL;

  let grid = makeGrid();
  let running = false;
  let fps = 12;
  let lastTick = 0;

  function makeGrid() {
    return Array.from({ length: ROWS }, () => new Uint8Array(COLS));
  }

  function step() {
    const next = makeGrid();
    for (let r = 0; r < ROWS; r++) {
      for (let c = 0; c < COLS; c++) {
        let n = 0;
        for (let dr = -1; dr <= 1; dr++) {
          for (let dc = -1; dc <= 1; dc++) {
            if (dr === 0 && dc === 0) continue;
            const rr = (r + dr + ROWS) % ROWS;
            const cc = (c + dc + COLS) % COLS;
            n += grid[rr][cc];
          }
        }
        const alive = grid[r][c];
        next[r][c] = (alive && (n === 2 || n === 3)) || (!alive && n === 3) ? 1 : 0;
      }
    }
    grid = next;
  }

  function bgColor() {
    // Active theme background — tracks both OS preference and the manual toggle.
    return getComputedStyle(document.body).getPropertyValue('--bs-body-bg').trim() || '#fafafa';
  }

  function draw() {
    ctx.fillStyle = bgColor();
    ctx.fillRect(0, 0, canvas.width, canvas.height);
    ctx.fillStyle = ALIVE;
    for (let r = 0; r < ROWS; r++) {
      for (let c = 0; c < COLS; c++) {
        if (grid[r][c]) ctx.fillRect(c * CELL + 1, r * CELL + 1, CELL - 2, CELL - 2);
      }
    }
  }

  function loop(t) {
    if (running && t - lastTick > 1000 / fps) {
      step();
      lastTick = t;
    }
    draw();
    requestAnimationFrame(loop);
  }

  // ---- patterns: each is an array of [row, col] offsets ----
  const PATTERNS = {
    glider: [[0,1],[1,2],[2,0],[2,1],[2,2]],
    lwss: [[0,1],[0,4],[1,0],[2,0],[2,4],[3,0],[3,1],[3,2],[3,3]],
    pulsar: [
      [0,2],[0,3],[0,4],[0,8],[0,9],[0,10],
      [2,0],[2,5],[2,7],[2,12],
      [3,0],[3,5],[3,7],[3,12],
      [4,0],[4,5],[4,7],[4,12],
      [5,2],[5,3],[5,4],[5,8],[5,9],[5,10],
      [7,2],[7,3],[7,4],[7,8],[7,9],[7,10],
      [8,0],[8,5],[8,7],[8,12],
      [9,0],[9,5],[9,7],[9,12],
      [10,0],[10,5],[10,7],[10,12],
      [12,2],[12,3],[12,4],[12,8],[12,9],[12,10],
    ],
    gun: [
      [4,0],[4,1],[5,0],[5,1],
      [4,34],[4,35],[5,34],[5,35],
      [2,12],[2,13],[3,11],[3,15],[4,10],[4,16],
      [5,10],[5,14],[5,16],[5,17],[6,10],[6,16],
      [7,11],[7,15],[8,12],[8,13],
      [0,24],[1,22],[1,24],[2,20],[2,21],[3,20],[3,21],
      [4,20],[4,21],[5,22],[5,24],[6,24],
    ],
  };

  function loadPattern(name) {
    const cells = PATTERNS[name];
    if (!cells) return;
    grid = makeGrid();
    let maxR = 0, maxC = 0;
    for (const [r, c] of cells) { if (r > maxR) maxR = r; if (c > maxC) maxC = c; }
    const rOff = Math.floor((ROWS - maxR) / 2);
    const cOff = Math.floor((COLS - maxC) / 2);
    for (const [r, c] of cells) grid[r + rOff][c + cOff] = 1;
  }

  function randomize() {
    for (let r = 0; r < ROWS; r++) {
      for (let c = 0; c < COLS; c++) grid[r][c] = Math.random() < 0.28 ? 1 : 0;
    }
  }

  // ---- input ----
  let painting = false, paintValue = 1;

  function cellAt(evt) {
    const rect = canvas.getBoundingClientRect();
    const x = (evt.clientX - rect.left) * (canvas.width / rect.width);
    const y = (evt.clientY - rect.top) * (canvas.height / rect.height);
    return [Math.floor(y / CELL), Math.floor(x / CELL)];
  }

  canvas.addEventListener('pointerdown', e => {
    const [r, c] = cellAt(e);
    if (r < 0 || r >= ROWS || c < 0 || c >= COLS) return;
    paintValue = grid[r][c] ? 0 : 1;
    grid[r][c] = paintValue;
    painting = true;
    canvas.setPointerCapture(e.pointerId);
  });
  canvas.addEventListener('pointermove', e => {
    if (!painting) return;
    const [r, c] = cellAt(e);
    if (r < 0 || r >= ROWS || c < 0 || c >= COLS) return;
    grid[r][c] = paintValue;
  });
  canvas.addEventListener('pointerup', () => { painting = false; });

  // ---- buttons ----
  const playBtn = document.getElementById('life-play');
  playBtn.addEventListener('click', () => {
    running = !running;
    playBtn.textContent = running ? 'Pause' : 'Play';
  });
  document.getElementById('life-step').addEventListener('click', step);
  document.getElementById('life-clear').addEventListener('click', () => { grid = makeGrid(); });
  document.getElementById('life-random').addEventListener('click', randomize);
  document.getElementById('life-preset').addEventListener('change', e => {
    if (e.target.value) loadPattern(e.target.value);
    e.target.value = '';
  });
  const fpsInput = document.getElementById('life-fps');
  const fpsOut = document.getElementById('life-fps-out');
  fpsInput.addEventListener('input', () => {
    fps = +fpsInput.value;
    fpsOut.textContent = fps;
  });

  // start with a glider, paused
  loadPattern('glider');
  requestAnimationFrame(loop);
})();
</script>
```

## Further down the rabbit hole

- [Conway's Game of Life](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Conway%27s_Game_of_Life) — Wikipedia: rules, history, and why a 4-rule cellular automaton is Turing-complete.
- [LifeWiki](https://conwaylife.com/wiki/) — encyclopedic catalogue of patterns. Still lifes, oscillators, spaceships, guns, puffers, eaters, reflectors — thousands of them.
- [conwaylife.com forums](https://conwaylife.com/forums/) — the research community is still active. New patterns get discovered every week.
- [Golly](https://golly.sourceforge.io/) — the high-performance simulator. Uses [Hashlife](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hashlife) to run universes that would crush the naive algorithm above by many orders of magnitude.
- [Paul Rendell's Universal Turing Machine](http://www.rendell-attic.org/gol/utm/) — a working UTM built inside Life. Glider streams as signals, glider guns as clocks, eaters as gates, all wired into a tape-and-state machine. Load it in Golly and watch it compute. The constructive proof that Life is Turing-complete.
- [0E0P metacell](https://conwaylife.com/wiki/0E0P_metacell) — Adam Goucher's self-replicating pattern (2018). A von Neumann universal constructor that copies itself from empty space, no background grid required. Decades after Life's invention before someone actually built one.
- ["Life in Life"](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xP5-iIeKXE8) — the Game of Life simulating itself, via the OTCA metapixel. Sit with this one.
- [Hashlife in Python](https://johnhw.github.io/hashlife/index.md.html) — John Williamson's pure-Python implementation. The quadtree memoization that makes Golly fast, in code you can read in an evening. (For production use: [python-lifelib](https://gitlab.com/apgoucher/python-lifelib), by the same author as the 0E0P metacell above.)
- [xkcd 2293](https://xkcd.com/2293/) — Randall Munroe's tribute to John Conway, who died of COVID in April 2020.

[← back to haigh.bio](/)

*Built by a glider, for a glider.*
